title: SpecKiwi performance indexing and read model implementation plan

phases.title: Performance contract and observability
phases.goal: Lock the SRS-scale performance contract and make read-path bottlenecks observable.

tasks.title: Add read-path instrumentation to perf harness
method_signatures: type PerfCounters = { cacheHit: boolean; parsedFileCount: number; artifactHitCount: number; fallbackReason?: string }
function recordPerfCounters(label: string, counters: PerfCounters): void
implementation_guide: Add cache/read counters to the existing timings JSON.
Keep strict budget values unchanged.
Use a helper that Phase P2 can later wire to real ReadModel stats.
tasks.rationale: Duration alone does not identify cache miss, YAML parse, artifact hit, or fallback causes.
pitfalls: Do not relax strict performance budgets.
tasks.test_guidance: Confirm output remains JSON-parseable and includes SpecKiwi performance timings.
dod: Local perf profile output contains counters
Strict profile budgets remain unchanged

rollback.command: 1) Restore test/perf/perf.test.ts from git. 2) Run npm test -- test/perf/perf.test.ts.

tasks.title: Add cold/warm DTO equality and filter baselines
method_signatures: async function expectColdWarmEqual<T>(label: string, cold: () => Promise<T>, warm: () => Promise<T>): Promise<void>
implementation_guide: Compare source path and warm cache path for search, requirement get, graph, and MCP search.
Compare requirement list project, scope, type, status, and tag filter combinations on source and warm cache paths.
Compare data payloads directly and diagnostics by stable code multiset.
Cover success, stale cache, corrupt cache, and filter-combination workspaces.
tasks.rationale: Read-path changes must not change public DTOs.
pitfalls: Exclude absolute temp paths and durations from equality.
tasks.test_guidance: Add success, stale, corrupt cache, and project/scope/type/status/tag filter-combination fixtures.
tasks.verification_command: npm test -- cache search graph mcp

acceptance_tests.cmd: npm test -- cache search graph mcp
npm test -- cache search graph mcp

dod: Cold/warm DTO equality baseline guards later phases

rollback.command: 1) Remove new equality helper and tests. 2) Run npm test -- cache search graph mcp.

phases.title: Cache manifest v2 and artifact IO
phases.goal: Make section freshness available without loading the whole YAML workspace.

tasks.title: Add workspace fingerprint module
method_signatures: export type SourceFileStat = { path: string; size: number; mtimeMs: number }
export type SourceFileFingerprint = { path: string; size: number; mtimeMs: number; sha256: string }
export async function statWorkspaceInputs(root: WorkspaceRoot): Promise<SourceFileStat[]>
export async function fingerprintWorkspace(root: WorkspaceRoot): Promise<SourceFileFingerprint[]>
implementation_guide: Walk only .speckiwi YAML files.
Use statWorkspaceInputs for hot-read manifest quick accept.
Run sha256 fingerprinting only on stat mismatch, cache rebuild, or artifact regeneration.
Use store path normalization and stable sort.
Exclude cache outputs and generated directories.
tasks.rationale: Freshness checks must not require LoadedWorkspace.
pitfalls: Do not hash 1,000 YAML files on every hot read.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover nested YAML, non-YAML exclusion, symlink escape, stable sort, and hot-read quick stat no-hash.
dod: Hot read quick stat path avoids loadWorkspaceForValidation and sha256 file hashing

rollback.command: 1) Delete src/cache/fingerprint.ts and its tests. 2) Run npm test -- cache.

tasks.title: Introduce manifest v2 with section freshness APIs
method_signatures: export type CacheVersionFingerprint = { speckiwiVersion: string; parserVersion: string; schemaBundleHash: string; tokenizerVersion: string; graphRulesVersion: string; dictionaryHash: string; searchSettingsHash: string }
export type IndexSectionName = "facts" | "entities" | "relations" | "search" | "graph" | "diagnostics"
export async function isIndexSectionFresh(root: WorkspaceRoot, section: IndexSectionName): Promise<boolean>
implementation_guide: Keep v1 manifest compatibility.
Add v2 type guard and section freshness helpers.
Include speckiwiVersion, parserVersion, schemaBundleHash, tokenizerVersion, graphRulesVersion, dictionaryHash, and searchSettingsHash.
Treat tokenizer, dictionary, and search settings changes as search stale.
Treat package, parser, and schema bundle changes as entities/relations stale.
Treat graph rules changes as graph stale.
Treat unsupported major version as stale.
tasks.rationale: Search, graph, and exact lookup require different freshness scopes.
pitfalls: Do not make search wait for graph output hashes.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover fresh, stale by file hash, package version change, schema bundle change, tokenizer version change, dictionary.yaml change, graph rules version change, unsupported major, and v1 fallback.
dod: Search section freshness returns without YAML parse or full-file hashing
Version, tokenizer, dictionary, and settings changes invalidate the correct sections

rollback.command: 1) Remove src/cache/index-manifest.ts and restore src/cache/manifest.ts. 2) Run npm test -- cache.

tasks.title: Add versioned artifact serialization helpers
method_signatures: export async function readArtifact<T>(root: WorkspaceRoot, storePath: string, guard: (value: unknown) => T | undefined): Promise<{ artifact?: T; warning?: Diagnostic }>
export async function writeArtifact(root: WorkspaceRoot, storePath: string, value: unknown): Promise<void>
implementation_guide: Restrict reads to fixed cache store paths.
Return warning diagnostics on parse or shape failure.
Write through atomicWriteText and stableJson.
tasks.rationale: All index sections need the same safe artifact read/write behavior.
pitfalls: Never trust path fields inside cache JSON.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover corrupt JSON, invalid shape, fixed path enforcement, stable write.
dod: Artifact read failures degrade to source fallback warnings

rollback.command: 1) Delete src/indexing/serialization.ts and related tests. 2) Run npm test -- cache.

tasks.title: Enforce bypass-first no-cache semantics
method_signatures: function shouldBypassCache(cacheMode: CacheMode | undefined): boolean
export async function rebuildCache(input: CacheRebuildInput = {}): Promise<CacheResult>
export async function loadReadModel(input: { root: string; cacheMode?: CacheMode; sections: IndexSectionName[] }): Promise<ReadModel>
implementation_guide: Check cacheMode bypass immediately after root resolution in rebuildCache.
Bypass mode in loadReadModel and searchWorkspace must skip artifact, manifest, memo, and cache writes.
Apply/write must not create stale markers in bypass mode.
Graph/export/read commands must not create .speckiwi/cache files in bypass mode.
Use directory listing tests to prove zero cache side effects.
tasks.rationale: SRS defines no-cache as an observable prohibition on cache reads and writes.
pitfalls: Bypass still permits source YAML reads; it only forbids cache artifact access.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover req update apply, search, graph, export, and cache rebuild bypass with cache directory side-effect assertions.
tasks.verification_command: npm test -- cache write graph export

acceptance_tests.cmd: npm test -- cache write graph export
npm test -- cache write graph export

dod: Bypass mode read/write commands perform zero cache artifact reads and zero cache artifact writes in tests

rollback.command: 1) Restore cache, read-model, apply, export, and graph-owned files from git. 2) Run npm test -- cache write graph export.

phases.title: Core API and immutable read model skeleton
phases.goal: Move orchestration into Core and add an immutable read model boundary.

tasks.title: Move SpecKiwi Core facade out of MCP adapter
method_signatures: export type SpecKiwiCore = { root: string; cacheMode: CacheMode; search(input: SearchInput): Promise<SearchResultSet>; getRequirement(input: GetRequirementInput): Promise<RequirementResult> }
export function createSpecKiwiCore(input: { root: string; cacheMode?: CacheMode }): SpecKiwiCore
implementation_guide: Move SpecKiwiCore and createSpecKiwiCore to src/core/api.ts.
Use core/inputs.ts types in Core signatures.
Keep MCP adapter responsible for registration and result conversion.
tasks.rationale: CLI and MCP must share the same read-model fast paths.
pitfalls: Do not change MCP tool names or InvalidParams behavior.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover direct core API, MCP tool call, and package export contract.
dod: src/mcp/tools.ts no longer imports loadWorkspaceForValidation directly

rollback.command: 1) Restore src/mcp/tools.ts, package.json, and tests from git. 2) Run npm test -- mcp contract.

tasks.title: Add ReadModel skeleton with source fallback
method_signatures: export type ReadModelLoadStats = { mode: "cache" | "source"; cacheHit: boolean; parsedFileCount: number; artifactHitCount: number; fallbackReason?: string }
export async function loadReadModel(input: { root: string; cacheMode?: CacheMode; sections: IndexSectionName[] }): Promise<ReadModel>
implementation_guide: Implement source fallback first.
Preserve sections argument for later cache-backed branches.
Return immutable snapshots and load stats.
tasks.rationale: A shared read model prevents command-specific fast-path drift.
pitfalls: Do not change output DTOs in this phase.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover source mode get/search/graph parity and bypass stats.
dod: Core call sites can opt into loadReadModel without output changes

rollback.command: 1) Delete src/core/read-model.ts and restore src/core/api.ts tests from git. 2) Run npm test -- cache search graph.

tasks.title: Preserve realpath security across Core and MCP reads
method_signatures: export async function resolveRealStorePath(root: WorkspaceRoot, storePath: StorePath): Promise<WorkspacePath>
export async function assertRealPathInsideWorkspace(path: WorkspacePath): Promise<void>
export async function readMcpResource(uri: string, core: SpecKiwiCore): Promise<ReadResourceResult>
implementation_guide: Core document read with raw or parsed output uses resolveRealStorePath before reading.
MCP resources keep resolveRealStorePath for overview, index, and registered documents after Core API move.
Apply keeps assertRealPathInsideWorkspace immediately before atomic write.
Cache artifacts use fixed paths and reject injected absolute or parent paths.
Security tests create symlink escapes for overview and registered documents.
tasks.rationale: Performance cache shortcuts must not bypass symlink and realpath hardening.
pitfalls: Logical store path validation alone does not stop symlink escapes.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover CLI read, MCP resource read, apply target write, and cache artifact path injection.
dod: Symlink escape attempts through Core read, MCP resource, apply target, and cache artifact path injection fail without exposing outside file contents

rollback.command: 1) Restore path, documents, resources, apply, and security test files from git. 2) Run npm test -- hardening mcp.

phases.title: Entity index and requirement exact lookup
phases.goal: Make requirement ID lookup a first-class cache-backed path independent of search and BM25.

tasks.title: Build entity and relation index modules
method_signatures: export function buildEntityIndex(registry: RequirementRegistry): EntityIndexV1
export function buildRelationIndex(registry: RequirementRegistry): RelationIndexV1
implementation_guide: Extract JSON-serializable documents, scopes, requirements, and lookup arrays.
Build runtime Maps only after deserialization.
Stable sort relation arrays.
tasks.rationale: Exact lookup cannot pay search index deserialization or document scans.
pitfalls: Keep full requirement payload out of the summary index.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover lookup ordinal, relations parity, and JSON shape guards.
dod: Entity and relation serialized indexes contain no Map, Set, or undefined

rollback.command: 1) Delete entity/relation index modules and tests. 2) Run npm test -- cache core.

tasks.title: Write entity artifacts and requirement payload shards
method_signatures: export type RequirementPayloadShardV1 = { format: "speckiwi/requirements-shard/v1"; documentPath: string; requirements: Array<{ id: string; requirement: JsonObject }> }
async function writeEntityArtifacts(root: WorkspaceRoot, registry: RequirementRegistry, sourceFiles: SourceFileFingerprint[]): Promise<void>
implementation_guide: Write entities.json, relations.json, and requirement shards.
Store only trusted shard identity fields such as documentHash and ordinal metadata in entity summaries.
Loader recomputes shard store paths from documentHash and rejects direct cache JSON path overrides.
Record section output hashes in manifest v2 while retaining legacy outputs.
tasks.rationale: Exact get needs full payload but should read only a target shard.
pitfalls: Never trust a cache-controlled payloadShardPath field.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover artifact creation, shard materialization, stale on deleted YAML, corrupt shard fallback, and cache JSON path injection rejection.
dod: Cache rebuild creates entity artifacts and manifest v2

rollback.command: 1) Restore cache rebuild and manifest files from git. 2) Remove entity artifact tests. 3) Run npm test -- cache.

tasks.title: Use entity index in getRequirement fast path
method_signatures: async function getRequirementFromEntityIndex(input: GetRequirementInput, model: EntityReadModel): Promise<RequirementResult | undefined>
export async function getRequirement(input: GetRequirementInput): Promise<RequirementResult>
implementation_guide: Check fresh entities/relations before YAML load.
Read only the target requirement shard on cache hit.
Fallback to existing full registry path on stale or corrupt cache.
tasks.rationale: This path directly targets the failed 50ms exact lookup budget.
pitfalls: Not-found must remain REQUIREMENT_NOT_FOUND.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover cache hit, includeDocument, includeRelations, corrupt fallback, strict perf.
dod: SRS-scale exact lookup is <= 50ms

rollback.command: 1) Restore requirements and read-model files from git. 2) Run npm test -- cache core.

phases.title: SearchIndexV2 serialized full-text index
phases.goal: Store and query real full-text index structures from cache.

tasks.title: Define SearchIndexV2 runtime and serialized shapes
method_signatures: export function buildSearchIndexV2(documents: SearchDocument[], dictionary: DictionaryExpansion): SearchRuntimeIndexV2
export function serializeSearchIndexV2(index: SearchRuntimeIndexV2): SearchIndexV2
export function deserializeSearchIndexV2(value: unknown): SearchRuntimeIndexV2 | undefined
implementation_guide: Add v2 envelope and keep v1 fallback.
Serialize exact map, filter buckets, postings, field lengths, DF, and dictionary.
Use JSON arrays for serialized maps.
tasks.rationale: Documents-only cache still rebuilds the runtime search index on cache hit.
pitfalls: Search result ordering must remain deterministic.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover serialization round trip, v1 fallback, invalid v2 fallback, stable output.
dod: V2 deserialization does not rebuild from full document list

rollback.command: 1) Restore search files from git and delete full-text module. 2) Run npm test -- search.

tasks.title: Switch searchWorkspace to cache-before-YAML path
method_signatures: async function loadSearchIndexFromCache(root: WorkspaceRoot): Promise<{ index?: SearchRuntimeIndexV2; warning?: Diagnostic; stats: ReadModelLoadStats }>
export async function searchWorkspace(input: SearchInput): Promise<SearchResultSet>
implementation_guide: Resolve root and check search section freshness first using stat quick accept, manifest section fingerprints, tokenizer version, dictionary hash, and search settings hash.
On fresh cache, deserialize search-index.json without loading YAML or hashing every YAML file.
On stat mismatch or version fingerprint mismatch, compute sha256 fingerprints and fallback to rebuild or source path with warning diagnostics.
tasks.rationale: Fresh cache search cannot meet 500ms if YAML parse is unconditional.
pitfalls: Fresh cache hit must have parsedFileCount 0 and must not hash every YAML file.
tasks.test_guidance: Assert cache hit stats and strict SRS performance.
dod: SRS-scale cached exact search is <= 500ms
Fresh cache hit has parsedFileCount 0 and fullHashCount 0

rollback.command: 1) Restore search/read-model/cache rebuild files from git. 2) Run npm test -- search cache.

tasks.title: Replace query-time full scans with filter buckets and postings
method_signatures: function candidateSetForFilters(index: SearchRuntimeIndexV2, filters: SearchFilters | undefined): Set<number>
function bm25SearchV2(index: SearchRuntimeIndexV2, queryTokens: string[], allowed: Set<number>, limit: number, offset: number): Bm25Candidate[]
implementation_guide: Intersect smallest filter buckets first.
Use exact map for exact mode.
Use postings for BM25 and materialize top-K results only.
tasks.rationale: After YAML load is removed, all-document scans become the bottleneck.
pitfalls: Auto mode merge must retain existing score and sort semantics.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover exact, multi-filter, BM25, auto merge, pagination.
dod: V2 runtime does not scan every document for filtered candidate selection

rollback.command: 1) Restore search algorithm files from git. 2) Run npm test -- search.

phases.title: MCP and CLI fast-path parity
phases.goal: Make adapters share the cache-backed read model and add safe memoization for long-lived MCP processes.

tasks.title: Route search, requirement, and list tools through Core API ReadModel
method_signatures: export function registerMcpTools(server: McpServer, core: SpecKiwiCore): void
SpecKiwiCore["search"]
SpecKiwiCore["getRequirement"]
implementation_guide: Import createSpecKiwiCore from core/api.
Route MCP search, getRequirement, and listRequirements through the same Core read model path as CLI commands.
Cover CLI/MCP search, get, and list parity for filters, project filter, pagination, and diagnostics.
Leave graph, trace, and impact warm graph parity to TASK-P6-003 after graph index exists.
Keep adapter-owned schema, InvalidParams behavior, root override rejection, and outputSchema metadata.
tasks.rationale: The SRS MCP budget measures MCP tool calls, not CLI-only calls.
pitfalls: Do not write protocol logs to stdout.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover MCP search/get fast paths, list project filter parity, CLI/MCP search parity, invalid params regression, and outputSchema regression.
dod: SRS-scale MCP search call is <= 1s in npm run perf:srs
CLI/MCP search, get, and list return matching Core DTO payloads for the same inputs

rollback.command: 1) Restore src/mcp/tools.ts and src/core/api.ts from git. 2) Run npm test -- mcp.

tasks.title: Add process-local ReadModel memoization with invalidation
method_signatures: export type ReadModelCacheKey = { root: string; cacheMode: CacheMode; sourceStatHash: string; manifestHash: string; artifactStatHash: string; sections: string[] }
export function clearReadModelMemo(root?: string): void
export function createReadModelMemo(): { get(...args: unknown[]): Promise<ReadModel>; clear(root?: string): void }
implementation_guide: Memoize by root, cacheMode, source stat hash, manifest hash, artifact stat hash, and sections.
Skip memoization for bypass mode.
Clear memo after apply, cache rebuild, and cache clean.
Recompute memo keys from cheap source stats and cache artifact stats on each request so external cache rebuild or clean invalidates the memo.
Use a small LRU cap.
tasks.rationale: Long-lived MCP processes repeatedly read the same fresh artifacts.
pitfalls: Memoized read models must be immutable snapshots.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover memo reuse, apply invalidation, cache clean invalidation, external cache rebuild manifestHash invalidation, corrupt artifact replacement artifactStatHash invalidation, and bypass no memo.
dod: Repeated MCP search records memo hits and write invalidation prevents stale response
External cache metadata changes invalidate memo without process restart

rollback.command: 1) Restore memoization-owned files from git. 2) Run npm test -- mcp write cache.

phases.title: Incremental facts and graph index stabilization
phases.goal: Add per-file facts and graph adjacency so validation and graph scale with the read model design.

tasks.title: Add DocumentArtifactStore and WorkspaceFacts
method_signatures: export type DocumentArtifactV1 = { format: "speckiwi/document-artifact/v1"; path: string; sha256: string; facts: DocumentFacts }
export async function loadDocumentArtifacts(root: WorkspaceRoot, fingerprints: SourceFileFingerprint[]): Promise<DocumentArtifactLoadResult>
export function buildWorkspaceIndexesFromFacts(facts: WorkspaceFacts): { entities: EntityIndexV1; relations: RelationIndexV1; search: SearchRuntimeIndexV2; graph: GraphIndexV1 }
implementation_guide: Key artifacts by source sha256.
Regenerate changed files only.
Load unchanged file facts from artifacts.
Rebuild cache by generating entities, relations, search, graph, and diagnostics artifacts from WorkspaceFacts.
Recompute global semantic diagnostics from facts.
Conservatively invalidate when index.yaml changes.
tasks.rationale: Per-file artifacts remove repeated YAML parse/schema work without risky partial BM25 patching.
pitfalls: Diagnostics cannot include absolute paths or timestamps.
rebuildCache must not keep depending only on full LoadedWorkspace traversal after facts are introduced.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover one-file change, index invalidation, corrupt artifact fallback, deterministic facts, and SRS-scale cache rebuild budget.
dod: One SRS file change regenerates one document artifact and keeps diagnostics correct
SRS-scale cache rebuild is <= 10s

rollback.command: 1) Delete document artifact and facts modules plus related tests. 2) Run npm test -- cache validate.

tasks.title: Move semantic validation rules to facts
method_signatures: export function validateWorkspaceFacts(facts: WorkspaceFacts): DiagnosticBag
export function loadedWorkspaceToFacts(workspace: LoadedWorkspace): WorkspaceFacts
implementation_guide: Extract duplicate, relation target, cycle, and reference rules into pure facts functions.
Keep validateRegistry as compatibility wrapper.
Sort diagnostics deterministically.
tasks.rationale: Validation, indexing, search, and graph should share one facts extraction layer.
pitfalls: Schema validation remains per-document.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover duplicate IDs, missing relation target, cycle, PRD refs, cold/warm diagnostics equality.
dod: Existing semantic behavior is preserved and facts warm path diagnostics are equal

rollback.command: 1) Restore src/validate/semantic.ts from git and delete semantic-rules tests. 2) Run npm test -- validate.

tasks.title: Add graph index adjacency and graph ReadModel section
method_signatures: export function buildGraphIndex(registry: RequirementRegistry): GraphIndexV1
export function graphResultFromIndex(index: GraphIndexV1, graphType: GraphType): GraphResult
export function traceRequirementFromIndex(input: TraceRequirementInput, index: GraphIndexV1): TraceResult
export function impactRequirementFromIndex(input: ImpactInput, index: GraphIndexV1): ImpactResult
implementation_guide: Build graph once from entity/relation indexes.
Serialize incoming/outgoing adjacency by edge key.
Use adjacency maps for trace and impact traversal.
Keep GraphResult as materialized DTO.
Route CLI graph and impact plus MCP graph, trace, and impact through the same Core graph read model path.
tasks.rationale: Graph calls should share the indexed read model and avoid repeated workspace loads.
pitfalls: Internal adjacency maps must not leak into Core DTO.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover graph type parity, trace parity, impact parity, CLI/MCP parity, and corrupt fallback.
dod: Warm graph/trace/impact path outputs match source path
Warm graph/trace/impact path avoids workspace load
Graph DTOs remain JSON-compatible

rollback.command: 1) Restore graph and read-model files from git. 2) Delete graph-index tests. 3) Run npm test -- graph mcp.
